title: centaurea transcaucasica subsp. alexandri
intro: Centaurea transcaucasica subsp. alexandri, also known as the Caucasian knapweed or Alexandrian knapweed, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Caucasus region. The medicinal properties of this plant have been attributed to its high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Centaurea transcaucasica subsp. alexandri include the potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
